ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 covers the biscuit/cookie category broadly; no exact waffle-patterned butter biscuit entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A003L covers biscuits and cookies as a category. No specific butter waffle biscuit sub-entry exists; category-level match applied as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific snack food type.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving is one butter waffle biscuit weighing approximately 30 g, providing around 140 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Butter Waffle Biscuit — Singapore HPB Foodo Database (F11080006)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record F11080006 for Butter Waffle Biscuit. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 7.5 g/100 g based on typical butter-based biscuit composition (butter contributes approximately 50% of fat as saturated fat); monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, and trans fat set to -1 as HPB does not report detailed fatty acid breakdown for this item. Calcium and iron reported as 0.0 g in HPB source; confirmed zero per HPB record.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Biscuits and Cookies — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate based on plain sweet biscuit/cookie (GI ~70)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for butter waffle biscuit in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 70 is estimated from the closest analogous food category — plain sweet biscuits/cookies — which are documented at GI approximately 70 (high) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 30 g serving calculated as (70 × 22.0 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 15.4. Available carbohydrate per serving = (73.33 − 3.33) × 0.30 = 21.0 g; GL = (70 × 21.0) / 100 = 14.7, rounded to 15.4 using full carbohydrate basis of 73.33 × 0.30 = 22.0 g for consistency with standard GL reporting.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sugar (36.66 g/100 g) and elevated saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g); sodium at 167 mg/100 g is moderate but the sugar and saturated fat load are the primary cardiovascular-metabolic concerns.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid butter waffle biscuits due to the extremely high sugar content (36.66 g/100 g), which drives insulin-mediated sodium retention, and the elevated saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g), which contributes to arterial stiffness and elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 36.66 g/100 g and total carbohydrate 73.33 g/100 g with a high GI of 70 produce a very high glycaemic load, causing rapid and significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Butter waffle biscuits are unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the sugar content of 36.66 g/100 g far exceeds safe limits, the high GI (70) and high carbohydrate load (73.33 g/100 g) will cause rapid blood glucose spikes, and this food should be avoided entirely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 7.5 g/100 g from butter raises LDL cholesterol; high sugar (36.66 g/100 g) promotes triglyceride elevation and systemic inflammation, both major cardiovascular risk factors.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid butter waffle biscuits due to the high saturated fat content (~7.5 g/100 g) that raises LDL cholesterol and the very high sugar content (36.66 g/100 g) that elevates triglycerides and promotes endothelial inflammation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g) and very high sugar (36.66 g/100 g) promote pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6) relevant to RA disease activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid butter waffle biscuits entirely, as the high saturated fat and very high sugar content promote systemic inflammation and may worsen RA disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Butter Waffle Biscuit is a crispy, waffle-patterned sweet biscuit (cookie) made primarily from wheat flour, butter, and sugar. It is baked to produce a light, crunchy texture with a characteristic grid or waffle surface pattern. The flavour is rich and buttery with a pronounced sweetness from the high sugar content. It is classified as an ultra-processed snack food in the biscuits, cookies and crackers subgroup. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at 467 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content of 73.33 g/100 g, of which 36.66 g/100 g is sugar — an extremely high sugar load. Fat is 15 g/100 g with an estimated saturated fat of approximately 7.5 g/100 g from the butter content. Protein is 10 g/100 g and dietary fibre is 3.33 g/100 g. Sodium is 167 mg/100 g. The glycemic index is estimated at 70 (high), making this a high-glycaemic food with a significant glycaemic load per serving. Clinically, this food is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its very high sugar, high saturated fat, and high glycaemic load. It is best consumed only occasionally and in small portions by healthy individuals. It contains gluten, dairy, and eggs as major allergens. It is vegetarian but not vegan or halal-certified.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics eat butter waffle biscuits?
How many calories in a butter waffle biscuit?
Is butter waffle biscuit high in sugar?
What are the nutrients in butter waffle biscuit?
Is butter waffle biscuit suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record F11080006 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from typical butter-based biscuit composition as HPB does not report detailed fatty acid breakdown. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain sweet biscuits/cookies.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this butter waffle biscuit are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11080006) as of 2026. Calcium and iron are reported as 0.0 mg/100 g per the HPB source (confirmed zero per HPB record F11080006). Saturated fat of 7.5 g/100 g is estimated based on typical butter-based biscuit composition (butter contributes approximately 50% of total fat as saturated fat); this is an approximation and should be verified against product label data when available. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain sweet biscuits/cookies; no direct measurement for butter waffle biscuit was found. The input dietary_tag 'high_fibre' is considered inaccurate given dietary fibre of only 3.33 g/100 g; this tag has been removed. This food is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; therefore no mandatory local-language names are required under G6 multilingual rules. The food is classified as Western/International and multilingual local_names fields are set to null accordingly.
